mission.title: Install and Configure Sermant on Kubernetes
mission.description: Sermant is a proxyless service mesh based on Java bytecode enhancement technology, designed to provide service governance capabilities in microservices architectures. Installing Sermant allows you to enhance your Java applications with governance features without modifying their source code.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Sermant
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Sermant to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy Sermant Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Sermant in the created namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check the status of the deployed pods to ensure they are running correctly.

uninstall.title: Remove Sermant Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the Sermant resources and the namespace to clean up the installation.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Sermant
upgrade.description: To upgrade Sermant, apply the updated manifests from the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n sermant`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the service is correctly defined and the pods are running. Use `kubectl get svc -n sermant` to check service status.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are in a pending state, check if there are sufficient resources available in your cluster using `kubectl describe nodes`.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Sermant will have all pods running in the 'sermant' namespace, and you will be able to access the backend service through the forwarded port. You can also verify the service governance capabilities in your Java applications.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have access to a Kubernetes cluster and have kubectl installed and configured.
